blocks.text: If the valve seat contact width is uneven or exceeds the limit, use a valve seat cutter to correct it. Valve seat contact width Intake: 1 Std. 1.05－1.35mm Limit value: 1 2.0mm Exhaust: (1) Std. 1.25－1.55mm Limit value: 1 2.0mm

items: Refer to the instruction manual of the valve seat cutter for the correction method.
The seat contact position should be in the center of the valve seat.

items.text: Refer to the instruction manual of the valve seat cutter for the correction method.
Refer to the instruction manual of the valve seat cutter for the correction method.
The seat contact position should be in the center of the valve seat.
The seat contact position should be in the center of the valve seat.

blocks.text: After correcting the valve seat to the correct contact width, apply compound evenly to the valve face surface as necessary, slide the valve with a valve wrapper, and inspect the contact with blue varnish.

items: Be careful not to allow compound to get between the stem and the valve guide during the sliding operation. After sliding, wash off the compound well, apply engine oil, and slide the valve wrapper again.

blocks.text: Measure the mounting height of the valve. Valve mounting height Intake: 1 Std. 49.05－50.05mm Limit value: 1 50.25mm Exhaust: (1) Std. 43.55－44.55mm Limit value: 1 44.75mm

items: If the limit value is exceeded, replace the valve and recheck. If the limit value is still exceeded during the re-inspection, replace the cylinder head.

blocks.text: Installation is the reverse of the removal procedure.
